title: NPS vs NPS Sanchay: Key differences between the two retirement schemes explained
summary: The National Pension System (NPS) is a popular retirement scheme in India. PFRDA introduced NPS Sanchay for informal sector workers, differing from regular NPS in eligibility and structure.

title: Inside ₹20-crore SME scam: Sebi bans 7 finfluencers — what investors should know before chasing online tips
summary: Sebi has uncovered a stock manipulation scam involving unregistered finfluencers who misled retail investors via social media, earning over ₹20 crore in illegal profits. The case highlights rising risks of social media stock fraud and the need for stronger investor awareness.

title: The hidden cost of carrying your home loan
summary: Equity investing will continue to create wealth into the future. But when the spread between borrowing costs and expected returns narrows, balance sheet strength is just as important as portfolio returns, if not more.

title: Gold fever in India: How higher import duty sparked a buying rush across jewellery, ETFs and digital gold
summary: If you purchased gold, how did the import duty change affect your gold purchase locally? The import duty prices are included by the jeweller in the gold prices, which also fluctuate based on other conditions.

title: Mutual funds are cutting exit loads. What does this mean for your investments?
summary: Fund houses across India are trimming or removing exit loads, the charges levied when you redeem your fund units before a specified holding period - usually one year - is up.

title: How households should prepare for a year of high inflation and muted income growth due to a faraway war
summary: The rising costs of everyday goods are hitting households hard, with global shipping bottlenecks and increased fuel prices as key culprits. Experts warn that this inflationary pressure will soon affect everything from grocery bills to loan interest rates.

title: 5 powerful ways to improve your SIP performance and build long-term wealth faster
summary: One of the biggest mistakes people make while doing SIP is sticking to the same amount for years. When inflation is rising, lifestyle changes, and salary increases, then you should also consider increasing your SIP contribution.

title: SIP can help you build a ₹1 crore education corpus for your child through early compounding — here’s how
summary: SIP investing for your child’s education can build a ₹1 crore corpus through the power of early compounding. Starting an SIP early reduces the monthly burden and ensures long‑term financial security for your child.

summary: Gold and silver prices gained on 25 May, with gold reaching ₹160,060 per 10 grams and silver rising to ₹276,830 per kg. The rally was supported by a weaker dollar and lower crude oil prices amid growing optimism over a potential US-Iran peace deal.
